title: tmuxcc session template
description:
A session template: a named, declarative session shape the driver can apply (at session-create, exactly once — or to a live session, safe-direction merge) and freeze back out of a live session (tc-gjdx). ONE shared schema, full tmux fidelity — the extension's managed subset is a semantic capability (apply-don't-reject), NOT a type wall.

The wire carries FULLY-SUBSTITUTED concrete templates: substitution (${workspaceFolder}, ${env:NAME}) is a client-side concern (tc-gjdx.6) and NO substitution syntax appears in this schema. Geometry is a structured split tree; tmux layout strings are a driver-internal compile artifact (tc-gjdx.3) and NEVER appear here or on the wire.

Vocabulary note: a template leaf pane names its shell command `command`; the driver's creating verbs (open-window / split-pane) name the same thing `shellCommand`. The compiler (tc-gjdx.3) maps template `command` -> verb `shellCommand` and template `env` -> the repeatable `-e` flag (D9-gated, tc-gjdx.2).

TemplatePane.description: A leaf node in the geometry tree: one pane. The sibling of shared/layout.json's LayoutPane, but DESIRED (a request), not ACTUAL: it carries NO paneId and NO Rect — the driver mints the pane and tmux sizes it. Every field is optional: an empty `{ "kind": "pane" }` is a default-shell pane inheriting the session working directory and environment.

cwd.description: Working directory for the pane (compiled to the creating verb's `cwd` -> tmux `-c`). Omitted: inherit the session working directory. A fully-substituted absolute path — no ${...} syntax.

command.description: Shell command to run in the pane (compiled to the creating verb's `shellCommand` -> the trailing tmux shell-command). Omitted: the default shell. A single shell-interpreted string, fully substituted.

env.description: Environment variables to set in the pane, name -> value (compiled to repeatable tmux `-e NAME=value`; D9 capability-gated per tc-gjdx.2). Omitted: inherit the session environment. Values are fully substituted — no ${...} syntax.

TemplateHSplit.description: A horizontal split node: children are arranged side-by-side (left to right). Mirrors shared/layout.json's LayoutHSplit and tmux's '[…]' notation, expressed semantically. A DESIRED split — no Rect; sizes are proportional, not absolute.

children.description: Child nodes arranged left to right.

sizes.description: Optional proportional weights, PARALLEL to `children` (same length, i-th weight for i-th child). Relative — only the ratios matter (e.g. [2, 1] or [0.66, 0.34] both mean 2:1). Omitted: an equal split. The length-equals-children invariant is enforced by semantic validation in code (JSON Schema cannot cross-reference two array lengths).

TemplateVSplit.description: A vertical split node: children are stacked top-to-bottom. Mirrors shared/layout.json's LayoutVSplit and tmux's '{…}' notation, expressed semantically. A DESIRED split — no Rect; sizes are proportional, not absolute.

children.description: Child nodes stacked top to bottom.

sizes.description: Optional proportional weights, PARALLEL to `children` (same length). See TemplateHSplit.sizes.

TemplateNode.description: Any node in a window's desired-geometry tree. Discriminate on `kind`: "pane" (leaf), "hsplit", or "vsplit". Arbitrary nesting is valid — expressive enough for anything tmux holds. The extension's strip-shaped managed subset (a single split of pane leaves) is a semantic capability, not a type restriction; wilder trees still apply (unmanaged, apply-don't-reject).

WindowTemplate.description: One window in a session template: an optional name plus an optional desired-geometry tree.

name.description: Optional window name. Also the merge key for apply-to-live (tc-gjdx.4): a live window with a matching name is left alone; a window whose name is absent live is created. Omitted: tmux names the window.

geometry.description: The window's desired split tree; leaves are pane specs. OMITTED: a single default-shell pane (the common 'just give me a window' case) — the compiler (tc-gjdx.3) treats a window with no geometry as one empty TemplatePane.

SessionTemplate.description: A complete session template: an ordered list of windows, with an optional identity name. Carried on the creating claim verbs (applied iff created:true), on the apply-to-live verb, and returned by freeze. Full tmux fidelity — validation is structural only; capability (managed vs unmanaged) is a downstream semantic, never a hard reject (apply-don't-reject).

name.description: Optional template identity. When applied, the driver records it as the `@tmuxcc-template` session user-option so 'created from template X' survives reattach (tc-gjdx.3 awareness). Also the config key under the extension's `sessionTemplates` setting (tc-gjdx.6). Omitted for inline/ad-hoc templates (no awareness stamp).

windows.description: The windows to create, in order. At least one — a template describes a non-empty session. (This is the INPUT template's window list; the apply-to-live would-create RESULT set may be empty when nothing is missing.)

TemplateApplyResult.description: Result of a `session.applyTemplate` command (tc-gjdx.4). In dryRun (preview) mode `windows` is the would-create set; on a real apply that SUCCEEDS `windows` is the did-create set — the two are equal for the same template+session (the preview-equals-apply invariant clients confirm against). A real apply that fails mid-transaction does NOT return this payload: it fails loud via the command response's error result (code + message naming the failed verb and the created-so-far state), with no rollback (tc-gjdx.3 partial-failure semantics).

dryRun.description: Echoes the request: true = preview only (nothing was created); false = the windows were actually created.

windows.description: The windows the safe-direction merge would create (dryRun) / did create (real apply): the subset of the template's windows whose names are absent in the live session. Empty when the template is already satisfied (a re-apply is a no-op). A would-create window IS a WindowTemplate (name + geometry).
